summary: The Docker-based reproduction could not be executed due to network connectivity issues in the build environment preventing package downloads. The vulnerability is confirmed through static code analysis: the API AddressController.update() method at line 98 uses request('id') without verifying the authenticated customer owns the address, and the web-based AddressController at line 111 properly checks ownership via $customer->addresses()->find($id), confirming the API controller is missing an equivalent authorization check.
expected_security_impact: An authenticated customer B should be able to modify customer A's address by sending PUT /api/customer/addresses/edit with id=<customer_A_address_id> and arbitrary address field values. The controller overwrites customer_id to the attacker's ID, stealing ownership of the address record.
actual_observed_result: Docker image build failed due to network connectivity issues (Unable to connect to ports.ubuntu.com:80 and GitHub clone timeouts). The reproduction environment could not be provisioned.
verification_method: The verification would check that: (1) the attacker customer's PUT request returns HTTP 200, (2) the response contains the modified address data with the attacker's chosen values, and (3) the victim customer's address list no longer contains the stolen address (ownership was reassigned).
evidence_produced_by_run: Static code analysis confirms packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Http/Controllers/API/AddressController.php line 98 uses request('id') without ownership verification.

The web-based AddressController at packages/Webkul/Shop/src/Http/Controllers/Customer/Account/AddressController.php line 111 correctly checks $customer->addresses()->find($id), proving the API controller omits the same check.
The AddressRequest rules() method validates field formats but never checks address ownership.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker image build failed due to network connectivity issues in the build environment (ports.ubuntu.com unreachable, GitHub clone timeouts).
The composer install step requires downloading ~200 PHP packages which requires reliable network access.
The reproduction bundle is correctly structured and should work in an environment with network connectivity.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub (https://github.com/bagisto/bagisto.git) at tag v2.3.9 and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
